title: dicoma anomala subsp. anomala
intro: Dicoma anomala subsp. anomala is a shrub-like plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea, dysentery, and stomach ulcers. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in managing conditions like fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. In traditional herbal medicine, Dicoma anomala subsp. anomala is prepared by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by making a decoction of its roots and stems.
